questions.question: Why does combining defineAsyncComponent with <Suspense> cause hydration flicker in SSR?
options: Async components are not supported in SSR at all
The async component's JS chunk may not be loaded when hydration starts, causing Vue to show the fallback
The fallback slot content is not serialized in the server HTML

questions.question: Why is using async setup() (top-level await) a better SSR alternative to defineAsyncComponent with Suspense?
options: async setup() components are excluded from hydration entirely
Nuxt serializes the fetched data in the payload, so hydration has everything without waiting for a chunk
async setup() components are rendered synchronously on the server
They skip the Suspense boundary during SSR

questions.question: In TanStack Query with Suspense, why must all useQuery calls come BEFORE any await?
options: TanStack Query uses a global registry that is not async-safe
Vue's composition context is lost after an await, so queries set up after it won't work correctly
await inside setup() cancels pending queries automatically
The Suspense boundary captures await calls but ignores useQuery
